title: Kanna Varuvayaa Song Lyrics Manathil Uruthi Vendum
stanzas:
Kanna Varuvayaa Song Lyrics Manathil Uruthi Vendum
#Ilaiyaraja #KJYesudas #KSChitra

Kannaa varuvaayaa
meeraa kaetkiraal
mannan varum paadhai
mangai paarkiraal
maalai malar cholai
nadhiyoram nadandhu

unnai ingu aadaipola
yaetru kollum poo udal

Meeraa varuvaalaa
kannan kaetkiraan
maalai malar cholai
nadhiyoram nadandhu
Meeraa varuvaalaa
kannan kaetkiraan

malligai panjanai ittu
melliya sittridai thottu
mogam theerkka vaa (2)

manmadha mandhiram solli
vandhanal sundhara valli
raagam saerkkavaa(2)

kodi idai odivathan munnan
madiyinil eduthidavaa
malarvizhi mayangidum vannam
madhurasam koduthidavaa

Kannaa varuvaayaa
meeraa kaetkiraal
mannan varum paadhai
mangai paarkiraal

enrichedMetadata.faq:
Frequently Asked Questions

What is the theme of Kanna Varuvayaa?

The song is devotional, expressing longing and spiritual yearning.

Who are the singers of Kanna Varuvayaa?

The song is beautifully sung by K.J. Yesudas and K.S. Chithra.

Who composed the music for Manathil Uruthi Vendum?

The music was composed by the legendary Ilaiyaraja.

What is the release year of the movie Manathil Uruthi Vendum?

The movie was released in 1987.

What emotions does Kanna Varuvayaa evoke?

The song evokes feelings of devotion, peace, and introspection.
enrichedMetadata.summary: Kanna Varuvayaa is a soulful devotional song from the movie Manathil Uruthi Vendum, released in 1987. Sung by the iconic duo K.J. Yesudas and K.S. Chithra, the song beautifully captures the essence of spiritual longing and devotion. The lyrics, penned by Vaali, combined with Ilaiyaraja's soothing composition, create an atmosphere of peace and introspection. The song is set against a backdrop of yearning and hope, making it a timeless classic that resonates with listeners seeking solace and spiritual connection.
enrichedMetadata.high_ctr_intro: Experience the divine melody of Kanna Varuvayaa from the timeless classic Manathil Uruthi Vendum. This enchanting duet by the legendary K.J. Yesudas and K.S. Chithra, composed by the maestro Ilaiyaraja, captures the essence of devotion and longing. Let the soothing tunes and heartfelt lyrics transport you to a realm of peace and introspection.
stanzaMeanings: Kanna, will you come?
Kanna, will you come? Meera is asking. The girl is watching the path the king will come. Walking by the river in the evening flower garden.
Kanna, will you come? Meera is asking.
Kanna, Kanna, Kanna...
I see the blue sky, the earth, and the water as you.
While eating and sleeping, I see your face.
Kanna, come and swim, the sea will burn and go away.
The flower body here will embrace you like a dress.
There is no other place like Brindavan. Even if dawn breaks, our embrace is heaven, isn't it?
Will Meera come? Kannan is asking. Walking by the river in the evening flower garden. Will Meera come? Kannan is asking.
Come to satisfy desire, placing jasmine on the bed, gently touching the waist.
Sundara Valli came chanting the love spell to join the melody.
Before the slender waist runs away, take it in your lap. Give the sweetness that makes the flower eyes dizzy.
Throughout the night in the rain of love, both bodies get wet in the moment.
One's poem is in the other's eyes.
Kanna, will you come? Meera is asking. The girl is watching the path the king will come.
Will Meera come? Kannan is asking.
Walking by the river in the evening flower garden.
Will Meera come? Kannan is asking.
Kanna, Kanna, Kanna...
